reasoning: Hans Niemann was ranked 15th on the FIDE October 2025 rating list, which forms the baseline for the October 26 Wikipedia rank. He played in the US Chess Championship in mid-to-late October 2025. Reports through round 9 (October 23) indicated he ended a "difficult stretch", hovering at or slightly under 50% in the first 8 rounds before beating Awonder Liang. Given the average rating of the field (around 2690) compared to his 2738 rating, a slightly positive or even score implies he likely bled several rating points (down to ~2735). The cluster around rank 15 is exceptionally tight (Dominguez at 2738, MVL at 2737, Ding Liren at 2738). Even a 2-3 point loss would likely drop him below these players, pushing his rank down to 17 or 18 (which numerically is HIGHER, therefore resolving NO). The November rankings will be released by FIDE on Nov 1. There is a small (~5-10%) chance Wikipedia won't be updated by Nov 2, keeping his rank artificially at 15 for the first date, which pads its probability slightly. By Nov 25, the new rankings will unambiguously rule him out if he lost points, lowering the probability. By January 2026, he has considerable time over two more FIDE rating lists to regain points and re-enter the top 15, leading to a reversion toward a 50/50 probability.
